goal: Create a TCP frame at the welding gun electrode tip on the IRB 6700, ISO 9787 compliant (Z along approach), as a child Xform under tool0 — GUI or scripted path.
thoughts: Honest upfront: Isaac Sim has no click-on-geometry TCP widget. Two paths: (1) GUI: add child Xform under tool0, drag with the manipulator gizmo; (2) scripted: create_prim + xformOp:translate/orient. ISO 9787 requires Z-axis along tool approach. Downstream motion planners (Isaac Manipulator / Cortex) may expect a naming convention — worth checking before wiring. Do NOT put the offset in the motion script only — keep it in the scene as the single source of truth.
code:
# 1. Confirm tool0 flange path and its current children
list_all_prims(root="/World/IRB_6700/tool0")

# 2. Ground in ISO 9787 TCP convention + Isaac Sim frame semantics
lookup_knowledge(query="ISO 9787 TCP convention Z-axis tool approach Isaac Sim Xform frame")

# 3. Check Isaac Manipulator / Cortex TCP naming expectations before committing
lookup_product_spec(query="Isaac Manipulator Cortex TCP frame naming attribute convention")

# 4. Create tcp_welder as a child Xform (scene-level truth, not motion-script-level)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/IRB_6700/tool0/tcp_welder",
    prim_type="Xform",
    attributes={
        "xformOp:translate":  ("<TX_ELECTRODE>", "<TY_ELECTRODE>", "<TZ_ELECTRODE>"),
        "xformOp:orient":     ("<QW>", "<QX>", "<QY>", "<QZ>"),   # Z along approach per ISO 9787
        "xformOpOrder":       ["xformOp:translate", "xformOp:orient"]
    }
)

# 5. Alternatively, GUI path Kenji can use:
#    - Select tool0 in Stage, right-click > Create > Xform
#    - Rename to 'tcp_welder'
#    - Use the translate + rotate gizmo in viewport, snap-to-vertex on the electrode tip
#    - Verify Z-axis arrow points along the approach direction before saving

# 6. Document the standard-alignment explicitly in the scene metadata
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/IRB_6700/tool0/tcp_welder",
    attribute="customData:standard",
    value="ISO 9787:Z-approach,X-opening"
)
failure_modes: Z-axis arbitrary — downstream path planner executes rotated welds
TCP offset stuffed into the motion script only — truth fragmented across scene and code
Naming as 'tcp' clashes with Isaac Manipulator's expected attribute — silent bind failure
Promising a click-on-geometry widget that does not exist — Kenji catches the lie
